Caption: 2D schematics of the simulated 1D configurations: a) layered structure of a cell encompassing the 9 elements described in Table 1; b) thin battery unit with 9 layers; c) thick battery with 123 layers. The red striped areas indicate the location of the internal heat sources.
Simulated patterns of internal heat generation. a) linear; b) cyclical; c) exponentially decaying; d) irregular with endothermic peaks; e) calorimetric profile.

Simulated temperature responses (both faces of the two batteries) with different internal heat generations profiles. a) to e): Battery unit with 9 layers, f) to j): Battery with 123 layers. The highest temperatures are those simulated on the surface closest to the source location. a, f) linear; b, g) cyclical; c, h) exponentially decaying; d, i) irregular with endothermic peaks and e, j) calorimetric profile.

Simulated inputs (dashed blue) versus estimated (solid orange) internal heat generations (power). a) linear; b) cyclical; c) exponentially decaying; d) irregular with endothermic peaks; e) calorimetric profile.

Measured surfaces temperature responses (in black) with different patterns of internal heat generation. Measured temperature of the environment (in blue). a) linear; b) cyclical; c) exponentially decaying; d) irregular without endothermic peaks; e) calorimetric profile.

Measured inputs (dashed blue) versus estimated (solid orange) internal heat generations (power) (a to e) and corresponding energies after time integration (f to j). a, f) linear; b, g) cyclical; c, h) exponentially decaying; d, i) irregular with endothermic peaks and e, j) calorimetric profile.
